name: Almenara and Marianet Pass Loop
name_original: La Plana Baja y el Alto Palancia — Vuelta desde Almenara
description: This moderate 38.0-mile road cycling route from Almenara explores the diverse landscapes of La Plana Baja and Alto Palancia, featuring Maria
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Almenara offer diverse terrain, from coastal paths to inland climbs. The region features a mix of paved roads, asphalt, and concrete surfaces suitable for road bikes. Cyclists can explore agricultural landscapes, natural wetlands like the Marjal i Estany d'Almenara, and routes extending towards nearby hilly areas. The varied topography provides options for different skill levels, including routes with significant ascents. Best road cycling routes around Almenara The most popular road cycling route is Almenara and Marianet Pass Loop, a 38.0 miles (61.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 1 minute to complete. This moderate route includes a notable ascent of 746 meters. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Eslida Pass – Eslida Pass loop from Almenara, a difficult 49.9 miles (80.3 km) path. This route features over 1,000 meters of elevation gain, traversing challenging terrain. Local road cyclists also love the Canet de Berenguer Marina – Marjal i Estany d'Almenara loop from Almenara, a 19.2 miles (30.9 km) trail leading through coastal areas and the Marjal i Estany d'Almenara wetlands, often completed in about 1 hour 12 minutes. Road cycling around Almenara is defined by varied terrain, including coastal roads, agricultural areas, and routes with significant climbs. The network offers options for both relaxed rides and more strenuous challenges. The routes in Almenara are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Almenara's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Almenara?
A: Almenara offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 10 recorded paths. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy rides to more challenging ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Almenara?
A: The Almenara region provides diverse road cycling terrain, including dedicated cycleways, quiet paved roads, and asphalt surfaces. You'll find a mix of coastal routes, agricultural landscapes like orange groves, and paths that extend towards nearby hilly areas such as the Sierra de Almenara, offering varied challenges and scenic views.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists in Almenara?
A: Yes, experienced cyclists will find challenging routes with significant elevation gains. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39565457" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eslida Pass – Eslida Pass loop from Almenara&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 80.3 km path featuring over 1,000 meters of elevation gain, traversing demanding terrain.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly road cycling routes in Almenara?
A: Absolutely. Almenara offers several easy routes suitable for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39565603" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font Redona – Casa de la Pallaresa loop from Almenara&lt;/a&gt;, which is 30.6 km with minimal elevation, perfect for a gentle outing.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass through or near notable natural features and landmarks. You can explore the unique wetlands of the Marjal i Estany d'Almenara, or enjoy views from spots like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2679470" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Taiola, La Marjal d'Almenara&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features agricultural landscapes, such as orange groves, and historic sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2756864" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Almenara?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Almenara are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular moderate circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e883086851/almenara-and-marianet-pass-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almenara and Marianet Pass Loop&lt;/a&gt;, covering 61.2 km with a notable ascent.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Almenara?
A: The road cycling routes in Almenara are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, which includes both coastal roads and routes with significant climbs, offering options for all skill levels.

Q: Can I find routes that explore the Marjal i Estany d'Almenara wetlands?
A: Yes, there are routes that specifically lead through the Marjal i Estany d'Almenara wetlands. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39564791" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canet de Berenguer Marina – Marjal i Estany d'Almenara loop from Almenara&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 30.9 km trail that takes you through this unique natural area.

Q: Are there routes that pass through charming villages or historical sites?
A: Many routes offer opportunities to pass through charming villages and historical sites. For example, the region's proximity to places like Castillo de Torres Torres suggests that longer rides could incorporate such cultural points of interest. You can also find routes near historical landmarks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3954829" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sagunto Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the typical length of road cycling routes around Almenara?
A: The road cycling routes around Almenara vary in length to suit different preferences. You can find shorter routes, such as the 30.6 km &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39565603" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font Redona – Casa de la Pallaresa loop from Almenara&lt;/a&gt;, or longer, more challenging rides like the 80.3 km &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39565457" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eslida Pass – Eslida Pass loop from Almenara&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there routes with moderate difficulty for intermediate cyclists?
A: Intermediate cyclists will find several moderate routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e883086851/almenara-and-marianet-pass-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almenara and Marianet Pass Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice, covering 61.2 km with 746 meters of ascent, offering a good balance of distance and climbing.
tips:
One of the ports of access to the Serra d'Espada that allows linking with the port of Eslida.
It is an ideal section for warming up, it does not have demanding slopes, the pavement is in good condition and there is usually little traffic. Clarify that in summer the sun will hit a lot because there is no shade on the route.

faqs.question: What is the typical duration for cycling this route?
faqs.answer: This moderate road cycling route typically takes a little over three hours to complete. However, your actual duration may vary depending on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a moderate road ride. While it's not overly extreme, it does require good fitness due to its length of approximately 61.2 kilometers and an elevation gain of about 750 meters. Beginners might find it challenging, especially the climbs.

faqs.question: Is this a loop route, or out-and-back?
faqs.answer: The route is designed as a return trip to Almenara, suggesting a loop-like experience. It starts and finishes in Almenara, taking you through the diverse landscapes of La Plana Baja and Alto Palancia before returning.

faqs.question: Where does the route start and finish?
faqs.answer: The route is named 'Return from Almenara', indicating that it typically starts and finishes in the town of Almenara. You can plan your journey to begin from a convenient point within or near Almenara.

faqs.question: Where can I park in Almenara for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: Almenara offers various parking options. You can look for parking in the town center or near Playa Almenara, depending on your preferred starting point. It's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter varied terrain on this route. While surfaces are generally well-paved for road cycling, some segments might be unpaved and more challenging. Expect gentle climbs, steeper ascents like the Puerto del Marianet, and rewarding descents and flatter sections as you approach sea level.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and natural features will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route showcases a beautiful blend of natural and agricultural landscapes. You'll cycle through orange groves, ancestral olive and almond groves, and areas with springs, ravines, and reservoirs. The Alto Palancia region, nestled between the Natural Parks of Calderona and Sierra Espadán, offers stunning valleys and mountains. Sections near the coast may also provide views of the Mediterranean.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is typically during spring and autumn when the weather is milder and more pleasant. Summer can be very hot, especially in the inland sections, while winter might bring cooler temperatures and occasional rain. Always check the seasonal weather conditions for Almenara and the Alto Palancia region before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route features several points of interest. You'll pass through the challenging yet scenic Marianet Pass. Other notable aspects include the old defensive tower of Algar del Palancia and the historic towns of Azuébar and La Vall d'Uixó, which add a cultural dimension to your ride.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with El Camí IT5TR8 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana, La Conquista de Valencia, and parts of the Vía Verde Ojos Negros, among others.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, and while dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, it's not typically considered 'dog-friendly' in the same way a hiking trail might be. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure they are safely secured in a bike trailer or basket, and always adhere to local leash laws and regulations, especially in towns or protected areas.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to cycle this route, especially through Serra d'Espadà Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a specific permit or to pay an entrance fee to cycle this route. While parts of the broader Alto Palancia region are near or within the influence of the Serra d'Espadà Natural Park, road cycling on public roads typically does not require special permits. However, always respect natural park regulations regarding waste, noise, and protected areas.
